title: Puerto Banus Dolphin Watching Catamaran Hire Costs and Tips 2026
html:
Dolphin watching catamaran hire in Puerto Banus offers a superb way to explore the Mediterranean charter scene, with shared tours from €60 and private skippered charters starting at €1,200 per day. BoatHire24 connects you with verified boat owners and operators right here on the Costa del Sol, making it simple to compare options and book with confidence.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

Most dolphin watching catamarans depart from Puerto Banus marina itself, a glamorous harbour lined with superyachts and designer boutiques. Other launch points include Puerto Jose Banus (the main port), Muelle de Levante for smaller vessels, and the nearby Puerto de Marbella just 4 nautical miles east. For a quieter start, consider Puerto de la Duquesa to the west or the sheltered Bahía de Estepona. Each offers easy access to the striped dolphin and common dolphin hotspots off the coast.

Which boat to rent

Choose the vessel that matches your group and budget. Here are the most popular options for dolphin watching.

  1. Catamaran, stable and spacious, ideal for families, up to 12 guests, from €1,200 full-day charter. The Leopard 51, for example, is a favourite. For full details, visit the Sanyati charter page.
  2. Motor yacht, faster and more agile, up to 10 guests, from €1,500 half-day. Popular models include the Azimut 55 and Fairline Squadron.
  3. RIB, thrilling ride for up to 6 guests, from €400 half-day, great for getting close to dolphins.
  4. Sailboat, quieter and eco-friendly, up to 8 guests, from €800 full-day.
  5. Luxury superyacht, crewed, up to 20 guests, from €5,000 full-day.

How much does dolphin watching catamaran hire cost in Puerto Banus?

Prices vary by vessel type, duration, and season. The table below gives typical euro ranges for 2026.

  • Shared dolphin watching tour: €60, €90 per person, 2, 3 hours, no licence required.
  • Small motorboat half-day: €400, €700 for up to 6 guests, 4 hours, skipper included.
  • Catamaran full-day: €1,200, €2,500 for up to 12 guests, 8 hours, with crew.
  • Luxury crewed yacht full-day: €3,000, €7,000 for up to 12 guests, all inclusive.

For the best deals on skippered options, browse skippered charters in Puerto Banus online. Most listings include clear pricing, photos, and availability calendars.

Do you need a licence to rent a catamaran in Puerto Banus?

No licence is required for skippered charters, but bareboat hire demands a valid ICC or equivalent. Spanish regulations require a recreational navigation licence for private boat operation without a skipper. For dolphin watching catamarans, most visitors opt for a crewed trip, where the operator provides all paperwork. Always confirm licence requirements directly with the owner when booking through BoatHire24, as local laws can change.

Tip: book your catamaran for early morning (8.30, 10.00) when the sea is calmest. In summer, the Levante wind picks up after midday, making conditions choppier.

When is the best season for dolphin watching in Puerto Banus?

The peak season runs from June to September, with warm sea temperatures and frequent dolphin sightings. High season brings bustling marinas, but also the best weather. In July and August, Poniente winds blow gently from the west, creating ideal flat seas for spotting pods of striped and common dolphins. Spring (April, May) and autumn (October) offer quieter conditions and lower prices, though dolphin activity remains high. Winter can be windy, but sightings still occur on calmer days.

At a glance

  • Price range: €60, €7,000 per trip depending on vessel and duration
  • Peak months: June, September
  • Trip length: 2, 8 hours
  • Guest capacity: 6, 20 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can I rent a catamaran without a skipper?

Yes, if you hold a valid International Certificate of Competence (ICC) and meet the boat owner's age and experience requirements.

What happens if the weather is bad on my booked day?

Most operators offer free rescheduling or a full refund if the captain deems conditions unsafe for sailing.

Is food and drink included in the catamaran hire?

Many private charters include a selection of soft drinks, beer, and a light lunch, but confirm with the owner at the time of booking.

How far in advance should I book a dolphin watching trip in Puerto Banus?

Booking at least two weeks ahead during summer is recommended, but last-minute availability exists outside peak months.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Puerto Banus or list your own. For 2026, the platform continues to connect travellers with verified owners offering everything from jet skis to superyachts. To browse all options or become a host, visit the BoatHire24 marketplace.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
